fixture_purpose: Single UK adult at Foundation life stage, age 18 exactly at fixture date. Exercises JISA-to-adult-ISA auto-conversion on 18th birthday, parent-account data inheritance with consent-to-link, LISA eligibility gate (not yet opened), auto-enrolment threshold boundary (earnings below £10k trigger), and Finio Score confidence-low path from sparse data set. No pension, no property, no protection. Tests engine handling of minimal-data entity and data-inheritance verification flow.

individual.name: Mr T Aged Out
individual.auto_enrolment_reason: Earnings £8,400 below £10,000 auto-enrolment trigger threshold; employer not obligated to enrol

investments.null_reason: FP-5 coverage: balance inherited from parent account; user has not independently confirmed post-conversion statement; engine holds at parent-verified value pending first user confirmation

bank.null_reason: FP-5 coverage: savings account opened in parent name for child; transfer to individual in progress; engine excludes from net worth until transfer confirmed and user verifies

fp4.expected_behaviour: income unstable at foundation stage; cashflow dimension uses range projection — conservative = current £8,400, optimistic = median first-job full-time salary £24,000; Finio Score confidence forced to low
no pension; engine generates pension-gap APQ item; state pension accrual = 0 years; timeline projection shows accumulation from zero if contributions started now
no protection; protection dimension score = low; APQ item: 'no life or income protection — consider income protection first'; engine does not penalise heavily at Foundation stage
sparse data set (< 3 asset categories populated, no protection, no pension); engine forces overall confidence = low; score bands have wide ranges

fp5.expected_behaviour: JISA/ISA balance inherited from parent account; engine uses parent-verified value as read-only anchor; APQ item generated: 'Confirm ISA balance post-conversion'; score shows amber confidence on capital dimension
savings account in parent name; engine excludes entirely from net worth until transfer confirmed and user provides first statement; APQ: 'Complete savings account transfer from parent'

archetype_specific.lisa_apq_note: LISA not opened; window open from today; APQ: open LISA before 40 for first-home or retirement; annual bonus 25% on up to £4,000 = max £1,000/yr government bonus being left unclaimed

auto_enrolment_state.engine_note: Monitor: if salary increases above £10,000, auto-enrolment triggered; APQ to fire on income update

state_pension_trajectory.note: No qualifying years yet; NI contributions not triggered at current salary

archetype_specific.data_sparsity_reason: Foundation life stage with inherited-unverified assets; no pension; no protection; one income source below threshold

finio_score.confidence_reason: Sparse data: fewer than 3 verified asset categories; no pension; no protection; employment income below NI/AE threshold; two inherited-unverified assets

behaviour.note: Partial data; no saving patterns yet established

capital.note: ISA unverified; savings excluded; very low verified capital base

tax.note: Clean: no tax liability, no complexity at this income level

protection.note: No protection in place; appropriate floor score for Foundation stage

cashflow.note: Income unstable; below AE threshold; wide range

debt.note: No debt; clean slate — maximum score for debt dimension

estate.note: No IHT concern; LISA not opened is sole gap at this stage

risk_score.confidence_reason: Foundation stage; minimal data; no protection; income below thresholds — all risk dimensions sparse

excluded_asset_reasons.savings-inherited: transfer from parent not yet confirmed; excluded until verified and transferred

net_worth.note: Includes ISA (unverified at £8,500 pending user confirmation) + current account £420 only

iht_projection.explanation: Estate far below NRB £325k; no IHT concern at Foundation stage

cost_of_inaction.explanation: Primary driver: LISA not opened — each year of delay costs up to £1,000 government bonus (25% on £4,000 max). Secondary: pension contributions not started — compound growth opportunity, especially significant at age 18.
